messages.content:
Describe me in detail this image please: visual + content. 

[Attached: test.png (image)]

The image is a system architecture diagram titled "Context Platform Example (and its own Knowledge Graph...)" on a light green background. It is organized into a left sidebar legend and three main horizontal layers: 1) Top Layer (Ontologies/Green): Features Enterprise, Project, and Consultant HR ontologies with interconnected nodes (e.g., Project, Consultant, Industry, Skills, Staffing rate) and relationship arrows like "works on" and "uses". 2) Middle Layer (Agent Catalog/Grey): Contains five AI agents (Project search, People Finder Agent, Staffing Agent, Training Suggessor, Performance Agent) represented by robot icons, with grey arrows pointing up to relevant ontology nodes. 3) Bottom Layer (Data & Analytics/Blue): Shows five data products (Cloud Storage Vector Store, Projects Knowledge Graph, Training DB, KPIs Dashboard, dbt Semantic Layer for KPIs) represented by database icons, with grey arrows pointing down from agents. A distinct blue arrow connects the Projects Knowledge Graph directly back up to the Project ontology node. Overall, it visualizes a cohesive platform where ontologies provide semantic structure, AI agents operate within this context, and data products store and retrieve information.

Linked: ontology2.pdf

Cached content added to conversation vector store
